sourceMovie.title: The Intern
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Intern for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Nancy Meyers keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Back to the Future
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Intern clicked for you, Back to the Future (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Intern?
faq.answer: Try Back to the Future, Back to the Future Part III, Lost in Translation—each shares DNA with The Intern while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Intern?
faq.answer: The Intern (2015) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Intern worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
